title: PC IBD stuck — diagnostic state
problem_one_liner: PC localHeight stays at 1 (genesis only) despite VPS at height 332. P2P connection works, heartbeat works, but blocks never apply locally.

what_we_know_works.evidence: peer.height = 332 on PC (was 22 stale before fix). VPS sees PC.height = 332 too.

what_we_know_works.item: Wire format V2 (32 raw bytes hash + 42 char miner_id)
what_we_know_works.evidence: Code reviewed line-by-line in p2p.zig. Both sides should use same format.
what_we_know_works.confidence: medium — assumes both binaries built from same commit

what_we_know_works.evidence: Port 18335 LISTENING (testnet RPC = 18332 + 1 because miner not seed)
VPS produces blocks alone (height 332) when PC silent — works.

what_we_know_broken.item: PC chain.dat stays at genesis (299 bytes, last modified 30+ min ago)
Miners panel in UI shows only 1 miner (dev wallet, 100%)
what_we_know_broken.explanation: PC chain has only genesis → savacazan never recorded as miner → dashboard reads VPS chain, sees only dev wallet

what_we_know_broken.evidence: User curl output: localHeight=1, peerHeight=332, progress=0, ibd=true

what_we_dont_know.question: Does PC actually send sync_request over the wire?
what_we_dont_know.how_to_check: log line: [P2P] SYNC_REQUEST trimis la <peer> (from height=1, max=2000)

what_we_dont_know.question: Does VPS receive sync_request and run buildHeadersResponse?
what_we_dont_know.how_to_check: VPS log: [DEBUG-SYNC-REQ] entered handler + [P2P] SYNC_RESPONSE trimis

what_we_dont_know.question: Does PC receive sync_response with N blocks?
what_we_dont_know.how_to_check: PC log: [P2P] SYNC_RESPONSE de la <peer> (X bytes)

what_we_dont_know.question: If PC receives blocks, why doesn't applyBlocksFromPeer apply them?
what_we_dont_know.how_to_check: PC log expects one of: [REORG], [CHECKPOINT], [SYNC] Gap in blocuri, [SYNC] Bloc #X aplicat. Need to see WHICH.

leading_hypotheses.name: Genesis hash mismatch (testnet simulation residue)
leading_hypotheses.description: User mentioned: '1000 mineri sintetici' simulation a few days ago. The PC's testnet genesis may have been generated with a different seed/timestamp than VPS's testnet genesis. They have different chain_magic but same merkle_root requirement at height 0 — if they diverge at genesis, applyBlocksFromPeer hits REORG_DEPTH_LIMIT and returns 0 silently.
leading_hypotheses.likelihood: HIGH — explains everything: PC stays at 1, VPS stays at 332, no error visible because [REORG] / [CHECKPOINT] log goes only when blocks arrive, and blocks may not arrive if peer.height > local but headers fail decode.
leading_hypotheses.test: Compare genesis.merkle_root on PC vs VPS via getblockhash 0 RPC OR check chain.dat size — VPS testnet should also be ~299 bytes for genesis-only fresh start.
leading_hypotheses.fix_if_true: Wipe PC's chain.dat completely + restart. Genesis will regenerate from chain_config.zig (deterministic). If it still mismatches, check chain_config.zig testnet genesis hardcoded values vs what VPS uses.

leading_hypotheses.name: Binary version mismatch (V1 vs V2 wire format)
leading_hypotheses.description: User rebuilt VPS binary recently with V2 wire format (32 raw bytes). If PC binary is older (V1, 32 ASCII chars), decode of MsgHeaders gives garbage merkle_root, prev_hash mismatch, all blocks rejected silently in 'Gap in blocuri' branch.
leading_hypotheses.likelihood: MEDIUM — depends on whether user did `zig build` on PC after VPS deploy. The fact that handshake works doesn't prove block format compatible.
leading_hypotheses.test: Check zig-out/bin/omnibus-node.exe modified time on PC. If older than commit 23487dd, rebuild.
leading_hypotheses.fix_if_true: cd 1_CORE/BlockChainCore && zig build (PC), restart HodLum worker.

leading_hypotheses.name: requestSync never called because peer.height race
leading_hypotheses.description: Initial PING sets peer.height=22 (stale VPS height when first connected). Heartbeat updates to 332 later, but `requestSync` may have been triggered only on .block_announce / .block_gossip events with the `peer.height >= local_len` guard. If no announcement arrives because PC isn't seen as validator by VPS (slot-skip), PC never gets nudged.
leading_hypotheses.likelihood: MEDIUM — would explain stuck progress=0 (sync_mgr.state.status=requesting but no actual outbound msg). User's previous fix to heartbeat suggests this path was already touched.
leading_hypotheses.test: PC log: count occurrences of '[P2P] SYNC_REQUEST trimis'. If 0, requestSync never fired. If >0 but progress=0, request fires but response empty/rejected.
leading_hypotheses.fix_if_true: Add periodic IBD tick (every 5s) that calls requestSync if behind, regardless of incoming events.

leading_hypotheses.name: Stale 1000-miner simulation data poisoning local chain
leading_hypotheses.description: User said: 'cateva zile in urma simulari cu 1000 mineri sintetici, acum sunt reali dar credut sa fie real'. Translation: a few days ago we ran simulations with 1000 synthetic miners; now they're real but [chain might be polluted from sim data]. The chain.dat may contain sim-era blocks that VPS rejects, and PC keeps trying to extend on a fork that VPS doesn't recognize.
leading_hypotheses.likelihood: HIGH — this matches user's intuition. If chain.dat has simulation blocks at heights 1-N, applyBlocksFromPeer sees first.height=1 < local_len AND hash mismatch → REORG path. If reorg_depth > REORG_DEPTH_LIMIT, returns 0 silently.
leading_hypotheses.test: Check chain.dat size on PC. If > genesis-only size (e.g. > 1KB), it has stale sim blocks. Also: check blockchain.dat in any other paths that may have been picked up.
leading_hypotheses.fix_if_true: Wipe PC chain.dat AND any other testnet data dirs. Restart with clean slate.

ranked_action_plan.name: Wipe PC testnet data — fresh start from genesis
ranked_action_plan.why: Eliminates H1 (genesis residue) and H4 (sim data poisoning) in one shot. Cheapest fix.
ranked_action_plan.risk: ZERO — testnet, no real value, regenerates genesis from chain_config.zig.

ranked_action_plan.name: Rebuild PC binary from current main
ranked_action_plan.why: Eliminates H2 (binary V1/V2 mismatch). User did changes today, needs rebuild.

ranked_action_plan.name: Restart HodLum worker with new logging
ranked_action_plan.why: Worker now writes to ~/.omnibus/worker-data/miner-testnet.log — we can grep for [P2P]/[SYNC]/[REORG] AFTER 30s.

ranked_action_plan.name: Read log + report findings
ranked_action_plan.why: If after wipe + rebuild it STILL stuck, log will tell us exactly which line in applyBlocksFromPeer rejects.

ranked_action_plan.name: If still stuck after wipe+rebuild: check VPS-side response
ranked_action_plan.command: ssh on VPS: journalctl -u omnibus-testnet -n 200 | grep DEBUG-SYNC-REQ
ranked_action_plan.why: Confirms if VPS even receives sync_request from PC. If no, issue is on PC outbound. If yes, response sent but PC drops.

files_already_modified_today: We-Are-Here-HodLum-WORKER.py — added log_fh writing to miner-testnet.log
MinerPanel.tsx — added syncing-self synthetic row (premature, user not using Tauri)
files_NOT_to_touch_yet: core/p2p.zig — applyBlocksFromPeer is sound, don't refactor without log evidence
core/sync.zig — buildHeadersResponse is sound, encoding is consistent

user_state.frustration_level: high — full day of debugging, going in circles
user_state.key_insight_user_provided: 1000 miners synthetic simulation a few days ago — chain may still carry that residue
user_state.what_user_wants_now: Concrete next step, not more code reading. JSON plan to track progress.

stop_doing: Reading more p2p.zig / sync.zig code without log evidence
Adding more debug prints before having basic log file
Modifying Tauri MinerPanel.tsx (user runs Python worker, not Tauri)
Speculating about HMAC / Sybil / PEX issues — those are deferred
next_message_to_user: Step 1 + 2 + 3 = 3 minutes. Wipe + rebuild + restart. Then paste log.
